architecture.summary: LoVer is an unsupervised verifier that operates on LLM hidden states to evaluate reasoning path correctness. For each reasoning path, it constructs contrastive assertions (e.g., 'This is a true answer' / 'This is a false answer') by appending templates to the solution. Hidden state features from these assertions are passed through a randomly initialized MLP with 2 hidden layers and sigmoid output to produce binary latent variables representing correctness probabilities. The verifier is trained without labels using three logical constraints (negation, intra-group, inter-group consistency) formulated as soft probabilistic losses. At inference, it scores assertions, groups them by final answer, and selects the answer with the highest group score.
trained_components: MLP verifier (2 hidden layers with ReLU activation)

biblio.title: Logic-Regularized Verifier Elicits Reasoning from LLMs

claims.dataset: 4 datasets (GSM8K, iGSM, HotpotQA, MMLU-Pro) across 3 LLMs
claims.statement: LoVer (sum) achieves an average absolute accuracy gain of 3.8 points over Majority Voting

claims.dataset: 10 datasets (4 main + 6 OOD from BIG-Bench Hard)
claims.statement: LoVer (sum) achieves performance comparable to the supervised verifier, reaching its 95% level on average

claims.dataset: 4 datasets (GSM8K, iGSM, HotpotQA, MMLU-Pro) across 3 LLMs
claims.statement: LoVer shows an average absolute accuracy gain of 7.8 points compared to CoT-Decoding
LoVer enables a 7B-parameter LLM to achieve reasoning accuracy comparable to that of a 32B-parameter LLM

claims.dataset: 6 OOD datasets: Boolean Expression, Web of Lies, Object Counting, Navigate, Multi-Step Arithmetic, Causal Judgment
claims.statement: On OOD datasets (6 BIG-Bench Hard tasks), LoVer outperforms Majority Voting with an average accuracy improvement of 10.3 points

Removing the entropy regularization loss leads to the most significant drop in reasoning accuracy, with LoVer tending to optimize towards assigning a score of 1 to all true assertions and 0 to all false assertions, losing the ability to discern correctness
LoVer relies on the hidden states of LLMs, which inherently restricts its applicability to white-box LLMs and prevents it from being directly utilized in black-box LLM scenarios

LoVer achieves highest reasoning accuracy when utilizing hidden states from the 15th or 20th layers of mistral-7b, aligning with prior research showing that middle to deeper layers contain richer knowledge
